Module com.example._0tazo
Package com.example._0tazo.model

Interface IPlayer

All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractPlayer, ComputerPlayer, HumanPlayer
public interface IPlayer
Contract that every player in the Cincuentazo game must fulfill, regardless of whether they are human or computer-controlled.

This interface defines the behavioral contract for player actions. Shared state and logic live in AbstractPlayer, which all concrete players extend.

Using this interface as the declared type in Game allows the game logic to treat human and computer players uniformly:


 LinkedList<IPlayer> players = new LinkedList<>();
 players.add(new HumanPlayer("You"));
 players.add(new ComputerPlayer("Machine 1"));
See Also:
  • AbstractPlayer
  • HumanPlayer
  • ComputerPlayer

    Description
    int
    chooseCard(int currentSum)
    Chooses the hand index of the card to play given the current table sum.

    Draws the top card from the main deck and adds it to the hand.
    void

    Eliminates this player: sends all remaining hand cards to the discard pile face down and marks the player as inactive.

    Returns the player's display name.
    int

    Returns the number of cards currently in the hand.
    boolean
    hasLegalMove(int currentSum)
    Returns true if this player has at least one card that can legally be played without the table sum exceeding 50.
    boolean

    Returns true if this player is still active in the game.
    boolean
    isFaceUpDraw()
    Determines whether cards drawn by this player are dealt face up.
    Card
    playCard(int index, Deck deck)
    Plays the card at the given index: removes it from the hand, sends it to the discard pile, and returns it so the caller can update the table sum.
    void
    receiveCard(Card card)
    Adds a card to the hand.

  • Method Details

    • chooseCard

      int chooseCard(int currentSum)
      Chooses the hand index of the card to play given the current table sum.

      For HumanPlayer, selection comes from the UI â€” this method returns -1 and the controller passes the index directly. For ComputerPlayer, this method applies the AI strategy.

      Parameters:
      currentSum - the current table sum
      Returns:
      the hand index (0â€“3) of the chosen card, or -1 if no legal move exists or selection is handled externally

    • playCard

      Card playCard(int index, Deck deck) throws GameException
      Plays the card at the given index: removes it from the hand, sends it to the discard pile, and returns it so the caller can update the table sum.
      Parameters:
      index - the hand index (0â€“3) of the card to play
      deck - the Deck whose discard pile receives the card
      Returns:
      the Card that was played
      Throws:
      GameException - if the index is out of range or the player is eliminated

    • drawCard

      void drawCard(Deck deck) throws GameException
      Draws the top card from the main deck and adds it to the hand. Whether the card is face up or face down depends on the concrete implementation.
      Parameters:
      deck - the Deck to draw from
      Throws:
      GameException - if both the main deck and discard pile are empty

    • receiveCard

      void receiveCard(Card card)
      Adds a card to the hand. Used during the initial deal.
      Parameters:
      card - the Card to receive

    • eliminate

      void eliminate(Deck deck)
      Eliminates this player: sends all remaining hand cards to the discard pile face down and marks the player as inactive.
      Parameters:
      deck - the Deck that receives the eliminated player's cards

    • hasLegalMove

      boolean hasLegalMove(int currentSum)
      Returns true if this player has at least one card that can legally be played without the table sum exceeding 50.
      Parameters:
      currentSum - the current table sum
      Returns:
      true if a legal move exists

    • getCard

      Card getCard(int index)
      Returns the card at the given hand index.
      Parameters:
      index - the hand index (0â€“3)
      Returns:
      the Card at that index, or null if not found

    • getHand

      ArrayList<Card> getHand()
      Returns a copy of the hand list.
      Returns:
      a shallow copy of the hand as an ArrayList

    • handSize

      int handSize()
      Returns the number of cards currently in the hand.
      Returns:
      the card count, between 0 and AbstractPlayer.HAND_SIZE

    • getName

      String getName()
      Returns the player's display name.
      Returns:
      the name of this player

    • isActive

      boolean isActive()
      Returns true if this player is still active in the game.
      Returns:
      true if active, false if eliminated

    • isFaceUpDraw

      boolean isFaceUpDraw()
      Determines whether cards drawn by this player are dealt face up.
      Returns:
      true if drawn cards should be face up
